steps.title: Spot the physical action
steps.content: The passage shows “I place my keys on the bowl” — the phrase “on the bowl” reveals the narrator is putting his keys in a specific location. That's the deciding clue.

steps.title: Match the action to the choices
steps.content:
The underlined word needs to mean set down in a specific spot — the narrator is putting his keys into the bowl. Substitute each choice:
  • "Put": "I put my keys on the bowl" — put means to set something in a particular position or location, which fits the action of depositing keys into a bowl. ✓
  • "Remove": "I remove my keys on the bowl" — remove means to take away from a location, the opposite of what the narrator is doing. ✗
  • "Rank": "I rank my keys on the bowl" — rank means to order or classify, unrelated to physically depositing an object. ✗
  • "Unfold": "I unfold my keys on the bowl" — unfold means to open out something folded; keys cannot be unfolded. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
